title: Keraselma lathyris
intro: Keraselma lathyris, also known as Lathyrus, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family, characterized by its showy, fragrant flowers and edible seeds. The seeds of the Lathyrus plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and are believed to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of the Lathyrus plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the seeds of the Lathyrus plant are often made into teas, capsules, or tinctures to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive problems and anxiety disorders.
